question: In which countries does Agriculture GDP make up a disproportionately large share of total GDP (millions of $)?

analysis.chart_type: Scatter plot: Agriculture GDP vs. Total GDP, colored by Agriculture % of GDP
analysis.design_rationale: A scatter plot allows for a joint view of both absolute (Agriculture GDP, Total GDP) and relative ('Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution') measures. Country points where the agriculture sector dominates (high % of GDP) stand out visually. Mapping color to % contribution reveals regional clustering or standout economies, while size exaggerates those with the largest agricultural sectors. This approach integrates multiple schema fields for a more nuanced story.
analysis.target_insight: Highlights countries where agriculture is not only a large economic sector in absolute terms but makes up a disproportionate share of the total GDP, distinguishing them from countries where agriculture is large but not dominant.

chart_encodings.color: Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution (continuous colormap)
chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: Alpha for overlapping points; log scale for axes; hover text for Country/Economy

transformations: Convert 'GDP (millions of $)', 'Agriculture GDP', and 'Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution' from strings to numeric floats, stripping non-numeric characters
Filter out rows with missing or zero values in any relevant field
Apply log10 transformation to 'GDP (millions of $)' and 'Agriculture GDP' for scale normalization
data_preparation_steps: df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'].str.replace('%','',regex=False), errors='coerce')
df = df.dropna(subset=['GDP (millions of $)','Agriculture GDP','Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'])
df = df[(df['GDP (millions of $)']>0) & (df['Agriculture GDP']>0) & (df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution']>0)]
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib as mpl
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c_squared\1.csv')
df['GDP (millions of $)'] = pd.to_numeric(df['GDP (millions of $)'].str.replace(',','',regex=False), errors='coerce')
df['Agriculture GDP'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture GDP'].str.replace(',','',regex=False), errors='coerce')
df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'] = pd.to_numeric(df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'].str.replace('%','',regex=False), errors='coerce')
df = df.dropna(subset=['GDP (millions of $)','Agriculture GDP','Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'])
df = df[(df['GDP (millions of $)']>0) & (df['Agriculture GDP']>0) & (df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution']>0)]
df['log_GDP'] = np.log10(df['GDP (millions of $)'])
df['log_Agriculture_GDP'] = np.log10(df['Agriculture GDP'])
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
norm = mpl.colors.Normalize(vmin=df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'].min(), vmax=df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'].max())
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,8))
sc = ax.scatter(df['log_GDP'], df['log_Agriculture_GDP'], c=df['Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution'], s=df['Agriculture GDP']/df['Agriculture GDP'].max()*300, cmap=cmap, norm=norm, alpha=0.7, edgecolor='k')
ax.set_xlabel('log10(Total GDP in millions $)')
ax.set_ylabel('log10(Agriculture GDP in millions $)')
plt.title('Disproportionate Share: Agriculture vs. Total GDP\nColored by Sectoral Contribution (%)')
cbar = plt.colorbar(sc, ax=ax, orientation='vertical', label='Agriculture Sector GDP of % Contribution (%)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
